person.name: Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer

nameForms.fullText: Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Matilda de Braose was the 2nd wife of Rhys Mechyll, son of Rhys Gryg and grandson of the Lord Rhys, Prince of Deheubarth.

Her parents were Reginald de Braose, Lord of Brecon and Abergavenny (c.1182-1228) and his wife Gwladus (Also known as "Gwladus the Dark Eye") Ddu (Daughter of Gwladus Ferch Llywelyn) (c.1189-1251)

Rhys Mechyll died in 1244 and some time after this Matilda placed the castle of Carreg Cennen in the hands of the Normans.

A Welsh chronicle, the Brut y Tywysogyon, records under the year 1248: "Rhys Fychan ap Rhys Mechyll regained the castle of Carreg Cennen, which his mother had treacherously placed in the power of the French, out of enmity for her son."
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matilda_de_Braose_(Deheubarth)

The wife of Rhys Mechyll was another Matilda de Braose, the grand-niece of the Matilda that had acted as protector of Maredudd's father.  This Matilda was the mother of an eldest son, Rhys Ieuanc ap Rhys Mechyll, who was perhaps 25 years old when his father died.  He had done something to earn his mother's enmity and she began to dispose of her husband's property when he first fell ill, just to deprive her son of it.  Hirfryn and Llandovery Castle had been put into the hands of a brother of Rhys Mechyll, Maredudd ap Rhys Gryg.  We cannot say exactly how Maredudd ap Gruffudd used his influence with the de Braose lady nor used his wealth in presenting his claim to the brother of Rhys Mechyll, but by about 1265 the old patrimony of Maredudd Gethin ap Lord Rhys came full-circle as the lordship and home of his grandson, Maredudd ap Gruffudd.
http://www.ancientwalesstudies.org/id213.html

zh.lifeSummary: 當Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer於1200年出生在英國威爾斯波斯布雷孔時，她的父親William de Braose IV26歲，母親Matilda de Clare19歲。她於1220年5月之前與Henry de Tracy, Lord Barnstaple結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1301年3月23日享年101歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Maude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Maud  , the usual spelling of the name in North America.

en.lifeSummary: When Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer was born in 1200, in Brecon, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, William de Braose IV, was 26 and her mother, Matilda de Clare, was 19. She married Henry de Tracy, Lord Barnstaple before May 1220. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 23 March 1301, at the age of 101.

Elle a épousé Henry de Tracy, Lord Barnstaple avant mai 1220. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 23 mars 1301, à l’âge de 101 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer 1200 in Brecon, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Braose IV 26 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Clare 19. Sie hat vor Mai 1220 Henry de Tracy, Lord Barnstaple geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 23. März 1301 im Alter von 101 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer è nata il nel 1200 nel Brecon, Powys, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Braose IV, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Matilda de Clare, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Henry de Tracy, Lord Barnstaple prima del maggio 1220. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 23 marzo 1301, all'età di 101 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Maude de Braose - Heiress of Tavistock Baroness Mortimer(이)가 1200년 영국 웨일스 파우이스 브레콘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Braose IV(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Clare(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1220년 5월 이전 Henry de Tracy, Lord Barnstaple(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1301년 3월 23일 101세의 나이로 사망했다.
